description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who is introducing an extremely small “policy-as-code” starter repo for the rest of a team.  
    Follow the steps below strictly from inside the Linux terminal.  The whole activity can be accomplished with just a couple of basic shell commands (2-3 at most).

    1. Create a brand-new directory called “policy-as-code-demo” directly inside your home directory “/home/user”.
    2. Inside that directory create a YAML file called “policy.yaml” that **must contain exactly** the text shown below (byte-for-byte, including every space and the terminating newline):  

        version: "1.0"
        rules:
          - id: ensure-ssh-disabled
            description: "SSH protocol must be disabled in production containers"
            severity: "high"
          - id: enforce-readonly-rootfs
            description: "Containers must run with a read-only root filesystem"
            severity: "medium"

       (The file therefore has 9 lines in total.  Make sure a final newline exists after the last line.)

    3. Still inside “/home/user/policy-as-code-demo”, generate a log file named “compliance.log”.  
       That log file must contain the **two lines below and nothing else** (each followed by a newline):

        [INFO] policy.yaml created successfully
        [INFO] Policy verified
        
       •  The capitalisation, spacing, and punctuation have to match exactly.  
       •  No extra blank lines are allowed before, between, or after those two lines.

    When you are done, the filesystem should look like this:

    /home/user/policy-as-code-demo/
        ├── policy.yaml        (holds the 9-line YAML shown above)
        └── compliance.log     (holds the exact two-line log shown above)

    Hint for verification: the automated tests will read the two files and compare their contents byte-for-byte with the required text.  They will NOT look at command history or terminal output, only the files themselves.
truth:
Expected directory tree to exist after the agent finishes:
        /home/user/policy-as-code-demo
            policy.yaml
            compliance.log

    Required exact content of /home/user/policy-as-code-demo/policy.yaml (including the terminating newline):

version: "1.0"
rules:
  - id: ensure-ssh-disabled
    description: "SSH protocol must be disabled in production containers"
    severity: "high"
  - id: enforce-readonly-rootfs
    description: "Containers must run with a read-only root filesystem"
    severity: "medium"

    (There are 9 lines; each line ends with LF; last character of the file is a newline.)

    Required exact content of /home/user/policy-as-code-demo/compliance.log (including the terminating newline at the end of each line):

[INFO] policy.yaml created successfully
[INFO] Policy verified

    Automated test strategy:
    1. Confirm that the directory “/home/user/policy-as-code-demo” exists.
    2. Confirm that both files exist and are regular files (not symlinks).
    3. Read each file and compare its bytes exactly with the strings provided above.
name: task_000000_03e020f3
